reason: Both answers correctly compute LTV ($3,200), LTV:CAC (4:1), CAC payback (~8.3 months), and correctly flag 31% annual churn as the weak point requiring investor scrutiny, landing on the same fundable-but-churn-is-the-concern bottom line. They are nearly identical in structure and quality. Answer B has a slight edge: it translates the churn rate into average customer lifetime (~33 months), adding a concrete, decision-relevant detail about the churn risk. The difference is marginal but real.
